fields.description: True if rpc service is enabled, false otherwise

statusFields.description: The device's Bluetooth MAC address with type suffix: ,p (public), ,rs (random static), ,rn (random non-resolvable), or ,rr (random resolvable). Example: "AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF,p"

(Since 2.0.0) When Bluetooth is active, contains one or more of: "scanning", "advertising", "connected". When Bluetooth is idle, this property is absent from the response. Status change notifications are emitted whenever the flags array changes.
Unix timestamp when pairing mode was enabled
Duration in seconds for which pairing mode is enabled
Duration of the current associations procedure in seconds
Unix timestamp of the start of the associations procedure

fields.description: Id of the CCT component instance
Name of the CCT instance
Output state to set on power_on. Range of values: off, on, restore_last
True if the "Automatic ON" function is enabled, false otherwise
Seconds to pass until the component is switched back on
True if the "Automatic OFF" function is enabled, false otherwise
Seconds to pass until the component is switched back off
Transition time (in seconds) - time to change from 0% to 100% of brightness (if applicable)
Brightness level (in percent) applied when there is a toggle and current brightness is lower than min_brightness_on_toggle.
Enable or disable night mode
Brightness level limit when night mode is active. null overrides night_mode.brightness with current brightness when night mode starts. Default value 50.

Color temperature level limit (in Kelvin) when night mode is active. null overrides night_mode.ct value with current ct value when night mode starts. Default value: 50% of ct_range. For DuoBulbG3: 4600

Containing 2 elements of type string, the first element indicates the start of the period during which the night mode will be active, the second indicates the end of that period. Both start and end are strings in the format HH:MM, where HH and MM are hours and minutes with optinal leading zeros
Controls how quickly the output level changes while a button is held down for dimming (if applicable). Default value 3. Range [1,5] where 5 is fastest, 1 is slowest
Brightness level (in percent) set on double click (if applicable). null overrides brightness with current brightness when preset is applied. Default: 100
Color temperature level (in Kelvin) set on double click (if applicable). null overrides ct with current ct when preset is applied. Default: max setting of ct_range (device specific)

Remaps output 0%-100% range to values in array (if applicable). First value in array is min setting, second value is max setting. Array elements are of type number. Accepted range for values is from 0% to 100%. Default values are [0, 100]. max must be greater than min.

Sets the color temperature operating range in Kelvin (if applicable). First value in array is min setting, second value is max setting. Array elements are of type number. Accepted range for values is from 1000K to 10000K. Default values are device specific. Default values for DuoBulbG3: [2700, 6500]. max must be greater than min.
Limit (in Amperes) over which overcurrent condition occurs (shown if applicable). For specific devices applies for color channels, not CCT component
Limit (in Watts) over which overpower condition occurs (shown if applicable)
Limit (in Volts) over which overvoltage condition occurs (shown if applicable)

statusFields.description: Id of the CCT component instance
Source of the last command, for example: init, WS_in, http, ...
Tag used to identify the origin of a state change
True if the output channel is currently on, false otherwise
Current brightness level (in percent)
Current color temperature level (in Kelvin)
Unix timestamp, start time of the timer (in UTC) (shown if the timer is triggered)
Duration of the timer in seconds (shown if the timer is triggered)
True if the output channel becomes on, false otherwise
Brightness level (in percent)
Color temperature level (in Kelvin)
Unix timestamp, start time of the transition (in UTC)
Duration of the transition in seconds
Temperature in Celsius (null if temperature is out of the measurement range)
Temperature in Fahrenheit (null if temperature is out of the measurement range)
Last measured instantaneous active power (in Watts) delivered to the attached load (shown if applicable)
Last measured voltage in Volts (shown if applicable)
Last measured current in Amperes (shown if applicable)
Error conditions occurred. May contain overtemp, (shown if at least one error is present)

fields.description: True if cloud connection is enabled, false otherwise
Name of the server to which the device is connected

statusFields.description: True if the device is connected to the Shelly cloud, false otherwise

fields.description: The numeric ID of the Cover component instance
Name of the Cover component instance
If True, all changes to physical inputs are ignored, regardless of mode.
Defines Cover target state on power-on, one of open (Cover will fully open), closed (Cover will fully close) or stopped (Cover will not change its position)
Watts, a limit that must be exceeded to trigger an overpower error
Volts, a limit that must be exceeded to trigger an overvoltage error
Volts, a limit that must be subceeded to trigger an undervoltage error
Amperes, a limit that must be exceeded to trigger an overcurrent error
configuration of the Cover motor. The exact contents depend on the type of motor used. The descriptions below are valid when an AC motor is used
Watts, threshold below which the motor is considered stopped
Seconds, the minimum period of time in idle state before the state is confirmed
Default timeout after which Cover will stop moving in open direction
Default timeout after which Cover will stop moving in a close direction
true when obstruction detection is enabled, false otherwise
The direction of motion for which the safety switch should be monitored, one of open, close, both

Watts, power consumption above this threshold should be interpreted as objects obstructing Cover movement. This property is editable at any time, but note that during the cover calibration procedure (Cover.Calibrate), power_thr will be automatically set to the peak power consumption + 15%, overwriting the current value. The automatic setup of power_thr during calibration will only start tracking power values when the holdoff time (see below) has elapsed
Seconds, time to wait after Cover starts moving before obstruction detection is activated (to avoid false detections because of the initial power consumption spike)
true when the safety switch is enabled, false otherwise
The direction of motion for which the safety switch should be monitored, one of open, close, both
true when slat control is enabled, false otherwise
Time it takes for slats to move from fully closed (0%) to fully open (100%) position, seconds. Must be manually configured by the user. Accepted range: [0.5..30]s
Time it takes for slats to move from fully open (100%) to fully closed (100%) position, seconds. Must be manually configured by the user. Accepted range: [0.5..30]s
Single step movement spread, represented as % from the full range (used only when slats are controlled via inputs). Accepted range: [1..100]%
Whether to retain slat position when cover (vertical) position is changed

This property only applies when cover (vertical) position is less than one full slat rotation away from the fully closed position. If this condition is met, any slat movement will always go through fully closed position first. This improves slat positioning accuracy, yet is slower to execute.

statusFields.description: The numeric ID of the Cover component instance
Source of the last command
Tag used to identify the origin of a state change

One of open (Cover is fully open), closed (Cover is fully closed), opening (Cover is actively opening), closing (Cover is actively closing), stopped (Cover is not moving, and is neither fully open nor fully closed, or the open/close state is unknown), calibrating (Cover is performing a calibration procedure)
Active power in Watts
Volts
Amperes
power factor
network frequency, Hz
Total energy consumed in Watt-hours.
Energy consumption by minute (in Milliwatt-hours) for the last three minutes.
Unix timestamp of the first second of the last minute.
Only present if Cover is calibrated. Represents current position in percent from 0 (fully closed) to 100 (fully open); null if the position is unknown

Only present if Cover is calibrated and is actively moving to a requested position in either open or closed directions. Represents the target position in percent from 0 (fully closed) to 100 (fully open); null if target position has been reached or the movement was canceled
Seconds, only present if Cover is actively moving in either open or closed directions. Cover will automatically stop after the timeout expires
Only present if Cover is actively moving in either open or closed directions. Represents the time at which the movement has begun
False if Cover is not calibrated and only discrete open/close is possible; true if Cover is calibrated and can be commanded to go to arbitrary positions between fully open and fully closed
Direction of the last movement: open/close or null when unknown
Temperature in Celsius (null if temperature is out of the measurement range).
Temperature in Fahrenheit (null if temperature is out of the measurement range).
Only present if slat control is supported and enabled. Represents current slat position in percent from 0 (fully closed) to 100 (fully open); null if the position is unknown
Only present if an error condition has occurred

fields.description: true if the connection is enabled, false otherwise
true if the inerface is configured to operate in server mode, false if in client mode
IPv4 mode. Range of values: dhcp, static. Applicable only for client mode. Can be omitted if once set
IP in the network for client mode static and for server mode enabled. Required if not already set
Netmask of the network for client mode static and for server mode enabled. Optional, defaults to 255.255.255.0
Gateway of the network for client mode static and for server mode enabled. Optional, set to null to clear
Nameserver to use for client mode static and for server mode enabled. Optional, set to null to clear
DHCP range starting IP address for the DHCP server in server mode. Applicable only for server mode. Required if not already set
DHCP range ending IP address for the DHCP server in server mode. Applicable only for server mode. Required if not already set

statusFields.description: IP of the device in the network

fields.description: Id of the Flood component instance
Name of the Flood instance. name length should not exceed 64 chars
Configuration of sound made on alarm. Accepted values are disabled, normal, intense and rain. When cable is unplugged value is null
Time waited before reporting flood status. For this period status must be steady. Accepted values are between 0-60 sec with step 5 sec

statusFields.description: Id of the Flood component instance
Alarm state
Mute state
Error conditions occurred. May contain cable_unplugged, (shown if at least one error is present)

fields.description: Id of the Humidity component instance
Name of the Humidity instance. name length should not exceed 64 chars
Humidity report threshold in %. Accepted range is device-specific, default [1.0..20.0]% unless specified otherwise
Humidity offset in %. Value is applied to measured humidity. Accepted range is device-specific, default [-50.0..50.0]% unless specified otherwise

statusFields.description: Id of the Humidity component instance
Relative humidity in % (null if valid value could not be obtained)
Shown only if at least one error is present. May contain out_of_range, read when there is problem reading sensor

fields.description: Id of the Illuminance component instance
Name of the Illuminance instance. name length should not exceed 64 chars
Threshold in lux. Accepted range is device-specific
bright threshold in lux. Accepted range is device-specific

statusFields.description: Id of the Illuminance component instance
Illuminance in lux (null if valid value could not be obtained) (if applicable)

Illuminance level interpreted according to dark_thr/bright_thr (null if valid value could not be obtained): lux below dark_thr is interpreted as dark, lux between dark_thr and bright_thr is interpreted as twilight, lux above bright_thr is interpreted as bright
Shown only if at least one error is present. May contain out_of_range, read when there is problem reading sensor

fields.description: Id of the Input component instance
Name of the input instance
Type of associated input. Range of values switch, button, analog, count (only if applicable).
Global enable flag. When disabled, the input instance doesn't emit any events and reports status properties as null. Applies for all input types

(only for type switch, button, analog) True if the logical state of the associated input is inverted, false otherwise. For the change to be applied, the physical switch has to be toggled once after invert is set. For type analog inverts percent range - 100% becomes 0% and 0% becomes 100%
(only for type switch, button) True if input-triggered factory reset option is enabled, false otherwise (shown if applicable)
(only for type analog) Analog input report threshold in percent. The accepted range is device-specific, default [1.0..50.0]% unless specified otherwise

(only for type analog) Remaps 0%-100% range to values in array. The first value in the array is the min setting, and the second value is the max setting. Array elements are of type number. Float values are supported. The accepted range for values is from 0% to 100%. Default values are [0, 100]. max must be greater than min. Equality is supported.
(only for type analog) Analog input range, which is device-specific. See the table below.

JS expression containing x, where x is the raw value to be transformed (status.percent), for example "x+1". Accepted range: null or [0..100] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.
Unit of the transformed value (status.xpercent), for example, "m/s". Accepted range: null or [0..20] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.
(only for type count) Counts report threshold in number of pulses. Accepted range [1 - 2147483647]
(only for type count) Reference time in seconds for base of frequency measurement. Accepted range [1 - 3600]
(only for type count) Frequency report threshold in percent. Accepted range [0 - 10000]

JS expression containing x, where x is the raw value to be transformed (status.counts.total and status.counts.by_minute), for example "x+1". Accepted range: null or [0..100] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.
Unit of the transformed values (status.counts.xtotal and status.counts.xby_minute), for example, "m/s". Accepted range: null or [0..20] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.

JS expression containing x, where x is the raw value to be transformed (status.freq), for example "x+1". Accepted range: null or [0..100] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.
Unit of the transformed value (status.xfreq), for example, "m/s". Accepted range: null or [0..20] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.

statusFields.description: Id of the Input component instance
(only for type switch, button) State of the input (null if the input instance is stateless, i.e. for type button)
(only for type analog) Analog value in percent (null if the valid value could not be obtained)

(only for type analog) percent transformed with config.xpercent.expr. Present only when both config.xpercent.expr and config.xpercent.unit are set to non-empty values. null if config.xpercent.expr can not be evaluated.
Total pulses counted.
total transformed with config.xcounts.expr. Present only when both config.xcounts.expr and config.xcounts.unit are set to non-empty values. null if config.xcounts.expr can not be evaluated.
Pulse counts for the last three complete minutes. The 0-th element indicates the counts accumulated during the minute preceding minute_ts. Present only if the device clock is synced.

by_minute values transformed with config.xcounts.expr. Present only when both config.xcounts.expr and config.xcounts.unit is set to non-empty values and the device clock is synced. null if config.xcounts.expr can not be evaluated.
Unix timestamp marking the start of the current minute (in UTC).
(only for type count) Measured frequency in Hz. Determined at every elapsed freq_window period.

(only for type count) freq transformed with config.xfreq.expr. Present only when both config.xfreq.expr and config.xfreq.unit are set to non-empty values. null if config.xfreq.expr can not be evaluated.
Shown only if at least one error is present. May contain out_of_range, read

fields.description: Set true to enable.

statusFields.description: The number of Matter fabrics that the device has joined.

true if the device can be joined to an existing Matter fabric. false means that the device is either already joined, or the commissioning window is closed due to timeout. In the latter case, the device should be restarted before adding to a fabric.

Enables publishing the complete component status on <device_id|topic_prefix>/status/<component>:<id> (<topic_prefix> when a custom prefix is set, <device_id> otherwise). The complete status will be published if a signifficant change occurred. Default value: false

Seconds to pass until the component is switched back on
True if the "Automatic OFF" function is enabled, false otherwise
Seconds to pass until the component is switched back off
Transition time (in seconds) - time to change from 0% to 100% of brightness or RGB levels
Brightness level (in percent) applied when there is a toggle and current brightness is lower than min_brightness_on_toggle.
Enable or disable night mode
Brightness level limit (in percent) when night mode is active. null overrides night_mode.brightness with current brightness when night mode starts. Default value 50.

Color level when night mode is active. Red, Green, Blue [r,g,b] - each value represents level between 0..255. null overrides night_mode.rgb array with current rgb array when night mode starts. Default value 255 for each color

Containing 2 elements of type string, the first element indicates the start of the period during which the night mode will be active, the second indicates the end of that period. Both start and end are strings in the format HH:MM, where HH and MM are hours and minutes with optinal leading zeros
Controls how quickly the output level changes while a button is held down for dimming (if applicable). Default value 3. Range [1,5] where 5 is fastest, 1 is slowest
Brightness level (in percent) set on double click (if applicable). null overrides brightness with current brightness when preset is applied. Default 100

Color level set on double click (if applicable). Red, Green, Blue [r,g,b] - each value represents level between 0..255. null overrides rgb array with current rgb array when preset is applied. Default value 255 for each color
Limit (in Amperes) over which overcurrent condition occurs (shown if applicable). For specific devices applies for color channels, not RGB component
Limit (in Watts) over which overpower condition occurs (shown if applicable)
Limit (in Volts) over which overvoltage condition occurs (shown if applicable)

true if the script runs by default on boot, false otherwise

true if the script is currently running, false otherwise

fields.description: Id of the Serial component instance
Operating mode for the serial port. Supported modes are listed in the modes attribute.
Baud rate
A 3-character string indicating the serial frame format to use, e.g. 8N1
Optional. Half-duplex mode. Only present on devices where the underlying hardware supports it. true = enabled, false = disabled.
Optional. Driver Enable (DE) active level. Only present on devices where the underlying hardware supports it. true = active high, false = active low.
RTU Server Address, between 1 and 247
Modbus RTU Client configuration. Reserved for future use.

deviceInfoFields.description: Id of the firmware of the device
Version of the firmware of the device
Name of the device profile (only applicable for multi-profile devices)
true if authentication is enabled, false otherwise
Name of the domain (null if authentication is not enabled)
Present only when false. If true, device is shown in 'Discovered devices'. If false, the device is hidden.

(since 1.7.5) Indicates the provisioning state of the device. Possible values: "pending" (initial unprovisioned state: open AP & BLE RPC are available for 15 minutes setup window, or 3 minutes on battery-powered devices), "confirmed" (first IP acquisition over Wi-Fi/Ethernet: open AP & BLE RPC are available for 5 minutes grace window; on battery-powered devices, the original 3-minute setup timer continues instead), "complete" (grace window expired: AP (unless protected with a password) & BLE RPC are disabled), "locked" (initial setup window expired without going online: AP (unless protected with a password) & BLE RPC are temporarily shut down until device is power-cycled, a factory/network reset occurs, or user button is pressed (latter only applicable to battery-powered devices)). See Secure provisioning for more details.
Cloud key of the device (see note below), present only when the ident parameter is set to true
Batch used to provision the device, present only when the ident parameter is set to true
Shelly internal flags, present only when the ident parameter is set to true

fields.description: Name of the device
Experimental Decreases power consumption when set to true, at the cost of reduced execution speed and increased network latency
Read-only base MAC address of the device
Read-only build identifier of the current firmware image
Name of the device profile (only applicable for multi-profile devices)
If true, device is shown in 'Discovered devices'. If false, the device is hidden.
Enable/disable addon board (if supported). Range of values: sensor(SensorAddon), sensor(ProSensorAddon), prooutput, LoRa; null to disable.
Enable/disable outputs toggle when the system (reset) button is pressed (shown if applicable).

(Since 2.0.0) When true, TLS certificate date/time validation is enforced for outbound connections. Can only be set to false when enhanced_security is false. This is a temporary option for users experiencing certificate validation issues due to time synchronization problems; subject to deprecation in future releases.

(Since 2.0.0) Read-only on devices shipped with firmware 2.0.0+ from factory (always true). On devices with factory firmware prior to 2.0.0, can be set to true but can only be reset to false via factory reset. When enabled, enforces additional security measures (see Enhanced Security section below).

True if enabled, false otherwise

Configuration revision. This number will be incremented for every configuration change of a device component. If the new config value is the same as the old one there will be no change of this property. Can not be modified explicitly by a call to Sys.SetConfig

statusFields.description: Mac address of the device
True if restart is required, false otherwise

Last time the system synced time from NTP server (in UTC), null when time is not synced from NTP server.

Size of the free RAM in the system in Bytes

BLE cloud relay configuration revision number, present on devices supporting BLE cloud relay functionality
BTHomeControl configuration revision number, present when device supports control with BLU devices
Version of the new firmware
Version of the new firmware
Period (in seconds) at which device wakes up and sends "keep-alive" packet to cloud, readonly. Count starts from last full wakeup
Time offset (in seconds). This is the difference between the device local time and UTC
Boot type, one of: poweron, software_restart, deepsleep_wake, internal, unknown.
Boot cause, one of: button, usb, periodic, status_update, alarm, alarm_test, undefined.

True if the device is operating in Safe Mode; present only in that mode. Not documented by Shelly.

fields.description: Id of the Temperature component instance
Name of the Temperature instance. name length should not exceed 64 chars
Temperature report threshold in Celsius. Accepted range is device-specific, default [0.5..5.0]C unless specified otherwise
Offset in Celsius to be applied to the measured temperature. Accepted range is device-specific, default [-50.0 .. 50.0] unless specified otherwise

statusFields.description: Id of the Temperature component instance
Temperature in Celsius (null if valid value could not be obtained)
Temperature in Fahrenheit (null if valid value could not be obtained)
Shown only if at least one error is present. May contain out_of_range, read when there is problem reading sensor

fields.description: Brightness of the device's screen while idle, in percent (0-100). Not documented by Shelly; sourced via device introspection.
Id of the Voltmeter component instance
Name of the Voltmeter instance. name length should not exceed 64 chars
Voltmeter report threshold in volts. Accepted range is device-specific
Input range, which is device-specific. See the table below.

JS expression containg x, where x is the raw value to be transformed (status.voltage), for example "x+1". Accepted range: null or [0..100] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.
Unit of the transformed value (status.xvoltage), for example "m/s". Accepted range: null or [0..20] chars. Both null and "" mean value transformation is disabled.

statusFields.description: Id of the Voltmeter component instance
Voltage in volts (null if valid value could not be obtained)
voltage transformed with config.xvoltage.expr. Present only when both config.xvoltage.expr and config.xvoltage.unit are set to non-empty values. null if config.xvoltage.expr can not be evaluated.
Shown only if at least one error is present. May contain out_of_range, read when there is problem reading sensor

fields.description: SSID of the access point (up to 29 symbols). Default is the deviceID, set to null to restore default. optional

True if the access point is enabled, false otherwise
True if range extender functionality is enabled, false otherwise

Password for the SSID, writeonly. Must be provided if you provide ssid
True if the network is open, i.e. no password is set, false otherwise, readonly

Ip to use when ipv4mode is static

Identical to sta. It will be used as fallback when the device is unable to connect to the sta network.

Interval at which to scan for better access points. Enabled if set to positive number, disabled if set to 0. Default value: 60
